text.title: Rate Limiting, Retry Logic, and Cost Tracking
text.summary: Rate limit tiers, mandatory retry logic for 529 errors, tenacity-based backoff pattern, and cost tracking by model including cache pricing.

rule.statement: Implement exponential backoff with tenacity for both RateLimitError (429) and overloaded_error (529) — 529 is not a client error; it is a transient server condition requiring retry.
rule.rationale: 529 overloaded_error is frequent during peak hours. Applications that do not retry it fail unnecessarily. Applications that do not back off amplify the overload.

example.note: APIStatusError with status_code >= 500 covers both 529 and other transient server errors.

rule.statement: For concurrent agent workloads: parallel subagents sharing one API key share rate limits — budget requests/min per subagent thread and monitor x-ratelimit-remaining-requests headers.
rule.rationale: Tier 1 limits (50 req/min) are hit immediately by even 3 concurrent subagents making rapid calls. Header-based throttling prevents cascading 429 failures.

rule.statement: Use the response.model field (not the requested model) for cost lookup — Anthropic aliases may resolve to a different model ID than requested.
rule.rationale: Cost tracking that uses the requested model string will misattribute costs when aliases resolve to different model IDs. The response field is authoritative.

rule.statement: Enable prompt caching (anthropic-beta: prompt-caching-2024-07-31) for repeated system prompts; cache reads are 90% cheaper than regular input tokens.
rule.rationale: Cache write is 25% more expensive, but cache read is 90% cheaper. For agents with large repeated system prompts across many calls, caching typically yields 60–80% cost reduction.
